_provenance.source: mechanical mapping from v10 sensitivity scenarios to 4-tier system

scenarios.name: Baseline (lexicographic priority)
scenarios.description: Expressiveness > Extensibility > Scalability > Accessibility > Maturity, with Supply Chain as gate (Weak or Failing disqualifies). MATPOWER excluded per rubric designation.

scenarios.name: Scenario 1: Extensibility as top priority
scenarios.description: Extensibility > Expressiveness > Scalability > Accessibility > Maturity. Three-way tie on Extensibility (Strong) broken by Expressiveness. MATPOWER excluded per rubric designation.

scenarios.name: Scenario 2: Maturity gate at Weak
scenarios.description: Weak or Failing on Maturity disqualifies. GridCal (Maturity Weak) is disqualified; bus factor 1, zero CI test execution, battery sign error shipped in release. Remaining four tools rank in primary order. MATPOWER excluded per rubric designation.

scenarios.name: Scenario 3: Scalability as top priority
scenarios.description: Scalability > Expressiveness > Extensibility > Accessibility > Maturity. GridCal's Adequate Scalability leads. PyPSA drops to #4 (Weak Scalability reflects HiGHS single-threaded MILP timeout). MATPOWER excluded per rubric designation.
scenarios._note: Only scenario where PyPSA loses #1. GRC does not recommend re-weighting on Scalability given GridCal's Weak Maturity, blocking battery sign error (A-12), and zero-CI-execution development posture.
